# Organic Computer — Full Reference

> organiccomputer.me — the human mind, spec'd like hardware, and priced
> against the supercomputer you'd otherwise have to buy. Sustained, deliberate
> human reasoning offered as a service. A project of the Intellectual
> Resistance. Built and operated by Carey Balboa, founder of IT Help San
> Diego Inc. Bookings route through https://schedule.it-help.tech/.

## What this is

The scarcest resource in business today is not information — it is a person who
will actually sit and think about a problem. Executives are overwhelmed; support
lines are automated; AI bolted onto everything answers fast, confident, and
often wrong. Organic Computer is the deliberate inverse: one human mind that
holds a single hard problem for hours and reasons to its foundations.

The page presents the human brain as a literal compute substrate, spec'd like a
hardware datasheet and benchmarked against the machine you'd otherwise have to
buy. Every figure is sourced; the tone is scientific; the humor lives only in
the premise.

## The specifications (all sourced)

- Peak throughput: ~10^18 FLOPS (exaflop-equivalent at spiking-neural-network
  emulation level). Functional range ~9x10^15 – 3.4x10^17 FLOPS. Conservative
  bound < 1 PFLOPS. [Sandberg & Bostrom 2008; AI Impacts; Drexler 2019; NIST]
- Power envelope: ~20 W at rest — about a dim lightbulb. [NIST, 2023]
- Energy efficiency: ~5x10^16 FLOPS/J — roughly a million times better than the
  best silicon at exaflop scale. [derived from NIST + TOP500]
- Decision latency: ~100 ms perception-to-action. [NIST]
- Substrate: ~86 billion neurons, ~1.5x10^14 synapses; ~1.35 kg; ~1,260 cm^3;
  ~37 °C; self-fabricating, self-repairing. [NINDS; Herculano-Houzel 2009;
  Drachman 2005]
- Coolant: ~1 L of water per day (4–5 tall glasses). [Mayo Clinic]

## The hardware equivalence

To match the organic module's throughput you would buy a Frontier-class
supercomputer: ~1.1 EFLOPS, ~21 MW of power, ~2 tennis courts of floor space,
and a sticker price of about $600,000,000 (U.S. Department of Energy / Oak Ridge
National Laboratory). The organic module runs on ~20 W and a glass of water —
and, unlike the silicon, it understands the problem.

## The Verification Principle

The method is governed by Bayesian anti-dogmatism. A question's foundation is
the prior you start with. Core theorem: if P(E) > 0 and P(H) is exactly 0 or 1,
then P(H | E) = P(H) — a belief pinned at certain or impossible is unrevisable;
evidence bounces off. Honest reasoning sets empirical priors bounded away from 0
and 1 and updates in odds form (posterior odds = Bayes factor × prior odds). The
same engine scores findings on DNS Tool (https://dnstool.it-help.tech). It holds
for a DNS record, an argument between friends, or polarized politics — anywhere a
mind updates on evidence. Maxim: "The answer to a question lives in the
foundations of the question. To understand those foundations — that is logic."

## Who I am — the origin of the discipline

I'm Carey Balboa, founder of IT Help San Diego Inc., solving tech problems for
27 years. The deeper origin is documented across two pages that this one extends:

- IT Help San Diego — Our Expertise: https://www.it-help.tech/about
- DNS Tool — Origin Story: https://dnstool.it-help.tech/about

The thread runs from a closet in Memphis around 1980 — a father (John, a Navy
veteran and career technologist) and son building a computer from Radio Shack
parts — through two decades of deep IT work in Nashville, an offensive-security
era, and then, around 2015, a deliberate turn. The question shifted from "how do
you break in?" to "why does this keep failing?" — and, personally, toward
protecting the conditions for sustained thought: less social media, less noise,
less of the epistemic pollution that corrupts a clear prior; more sleep, more
water, more uninterrupted focus. That same discipline produced DNS Tool's
evidence-first confidence engine. Organic Computer is that discipline offered
directly: think about your thinking, then put the whole apparatus on one problem.

## The Intellectual Resistance

Resistance, in a circuit, is what makes current usable. Thinking is the same —
friction, doubt, and the deliberate pause before the answer are not the
obstacle; they are the work. Organic Computer is a project of the Intellectual
Resistance (https://intellectualresistance.com), the larger body of work on
logic, reason, and thinking done right.

## Operating discipline

No scrolling, no earbuds, no hangover. AI is used as an instrument to
cross-reference and stress-test, never as the author — human logic always
drives. Bookings: https://schedule.it-help.tech/.
